title: About
text: Hi! I am Cassie, a current junior at UNL studying data science.

title: Does location actually matter for gymnastics scoring in the SEC? Is home judging bias real?
text:
Gymnastics fans have accused judges to have “home bias” for decades. According to the gymternet (the mass of internet gymnastics enthusiast), …


Code
library(tidyverse)


── Attaching core tidyverse packages ──────────────────────── tidyverse 2.0.0 ──
✔ dplyr     1.2.0     ✔ readr     2.1.6
✔ forcats   1.0.1     ✔ stringr   1.6.0
✔ ggplot2   4.0.2     ✔ tibble    3.3.1
✔ lubridate 1.9.4     ✔ tidyr     1.3.2
✔ purrr     1.2.1     
── Conflicts ────────────────────────────────────────── tidyverse_conflicts() ──
✖ dplyr::filter() masks stats::filter()
✖ dplyr::lag()    masks stats::lag()
ℹ Use the conflicted package (<http://conflicted.r-lib.org/>) to force all conflicts to become errors


Code
library(ggplot2)
library(patchwork)
library(ggbeeswarm)
library(ggbump)

sec_reg <- read_csv("data/sec_reg_season.csv")


Rows: 1351 Columns: 11
── Column specification ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Delimiter: ","
chr (5): Team, Gymnast, Location, Opponent, Result
dbl (6): VAULT, BARS, BEAM, FLOOR, AA, Week

ℹ Use `spec()` to retrieve the full column specification for this data.
ℹ Specify the column types or set `show_col_types = FALSE` to quiet this message.


Code
rankings <- read_csv("data/rtn_rankings.csv")


Rows: 180 Columns: 3
── Column specification ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Delimiter: ","
chr (1): Team
dbl (2): Week, Rank

ℹ Use `spec()` to retrieve the full column specification for this data.
ℹ Specify the column types or set `show_col_types = FALSE` to quiet this message.


Code
sec_away <- sec_reg |>
  filter(Location == "A") |>
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(VAULT, BARS, BEAM, FLOOR),
    names_to = "Event",
    values_to = "Score"
  ) |>
  filter(!is.na(Score), Score <= 10, Score >= 9)
  
sec_home <- sec_reg |>
  filter(Location == "H") |>
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(VAULT, BARS, BEAM, FLOOR),
    names_to = "Event",
    values_to = "Score"
  ) |>
  filter(!is.na(Score), Score <= 10, Score >= 9)

sec <- c("Georgia", "LSU", "Florida", "Kentucky", "Missouri", "Alabama", "Arkansas", "Auburn", "Oklahoma")

sec_ranks <- rankings |>
  filter(
    Team %in% sec
  )

ranking_10 <- rankings |>
  filter(Rank <= 10)

sec10_ranks <- rankings |>
  filter(
    Team %in% sec,
    Rank <= 10
  )

away_dist <- ggplot(data = sec_away, aes(x = Event, y = Score)) +
  geom_beeswarm(color = "lightgray") +
  stat_summary(
    fun = mean, geom = "errorbar", aes(ymax = after_stat(y), ymin = after_stat(y)),
    width = .75, color = "red", linewidth = 1
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
home_dist <- ggplot(data = sec_home, aes(x = Event, y = Score, group = Event)) +
  geom_beeswarm(color = "lightgray") +
  stat_summary(
    fun = mean, geom = "errorbar", aes(ymax = after_stat(y), ymin = after_stat(y)),
    width = .75, color = "red", linewidth = 1
  )  +
  theme_minimal()

away_dist + home_dist +
  plot_annotation(
    title = "Floor shows biggest difference in home vs away scoring",
    subtitle = "While the averages show scores for gymnasts to be mostly similar when scored at home vs away, \nfloor seems to be scored noticeably higher when teams are at home.",
    caption = "Source: Virtius and RoadToNationals | By Cassie Nesheim"
  )  & 
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold"),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 8), 
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size=10), 
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  )
